quotes.text: Sure he's not the real Santa at all, it's just a man in a costume.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong crouching down to comfort his youngest daughter Loretta who is afraid to sit on Santa's knee. Source: 2024 screenplay adaptation by Enda Walsh; verify against Keegan's novel before classroom citation.

quotes.text: He might be trying, we don't know.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong's quiet defence of Mick Sinnott (a local drinker whose son Furlong has just given a coin) against Eileen's dismissal. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Have you never questioned it?
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong to Eileen after his first encounter with the girl in the convent's Hall. The pivot of the play's central marital argument. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: But what if it was one of ours?
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong's response to Eileen's argument that the laundry girls are not their concern. The line that names the parallel. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Where would my mother have gone if it wasn't for her help? Where would I be without what she did for me?
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong invoking Mrs. Wilson's care for his unmarried mother as the decisive counter to Eileen's argument that the girls are not their problem. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: I have my mother's name and no harm came from that.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong's reply to Sr. Mary's pointed remark that he has no son to carry on his name. His fullest verbal defence of his illegitimate origin. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Come on, Sarah.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong returning to the coal shed at night, finding Sarah Redmond locked in, calling her by name. The moment of decision. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: It's not long now. We're almost home.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong walking Sarah Redmond up Chapel Lane to his own house. The film's final spoken line. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: If you want to get on in life, there are things you have to ignore.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen's central thesis statement to Bill in their bedroom. The line on which the marital argument turns. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: This is what I'm saying to ya, they are not ours.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen sharpening her position when Bill suggests one of the laundry girls could have been one of their daughters. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: It's far from hardship that you were reared, Bill.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen's accidental cruelty in the bedroom argument; she apologises within seconds but the line lands. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: You've always been soft hearted.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen describing the trait that worries her about her husband's exposure to the convent's situation. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Aren't they kept warm and fed and learning a trade? If it wasn't for the nuns they'd have nowhere to go.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen rehearsing the standard community defence of the laundries. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Sure his dad will only drink it.
quotes.speaker: Eileen Furlong
quotes.context: Eileen's response to Furlong giving the Sinnott boy fifty pence. The casual middle-class moralism of the rural town. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: We can't have people walking in and out whenever they want.
quotes.speaker: Sr. Mary
quotes.context: Sr. Carmel's line, echoed by Sr. Mary's institutional voice across the film. The convent's principle of opacity stated plainly. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel; note speaker may differ.

quotes.text: It must be a little disappointing all the same? To not have a boy to carry on your name.
quotes.speaker: Sr. Mary
quotes.context: Sr. Mary's pointed and unprompted remark while serving Furlong tea after the discovery of Sarah Redmond in the coal shed. Reads as a calculated taunt. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Don't be thinking that you're in any trouble. Tell us how you came to be locked in that shed?
quotes.speaker: Sr. Mary
quotes.context: Sr. Mary interrogating Sarah Redmond in front of Furlong, with Sr. Frances coaching the answer from the end of the table. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Will you take me to the river? Or get me to the other side of the gate.
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: The girl in the Hall on her hands and knees polishing the floor; she gets up and grabs Furlong's arm to plead. The moment that begins to dismantle Furlong's denial. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Don't leave me here! Take me home and I'll work for you, Sir, I swear it.
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: Sarah's escalation of her plea before she suddenly drops back to the floor and resumes polishing as Sr. Carmel approaches. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Is it the day or night, Mister?
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: Sarah's first words to Furlong on the morning he finds her in the coal shed. The mark of how long she has been locked there. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: I'll have my baby here. Not for three months they said.
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: Sarah explaining her situation to Furlong in the coal shed. The pregnancy that the institution has been concealing. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Sister Mary said it will go to a good home.
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: Sarah's flat, indoctrinated reporting of the convent's plan for her child. The line carries the institutional script intact. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Daddy didn't want me home.
quotes.speaker: Sarah Redmond
quotes.context: Sarah explaining how she ended up at the Good Shepherd. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Ya have to break the boy to make the man.
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Kehoe
quotes.context: Mrs. Kehoe's offhand line to Furlong after embarrassing his employee PJ at the food counter. The community's working philosophy in a single sentence. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Keep the bad dog with ya and the good dog won't bite, isn't that it?
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Kehoe
quotes.context: Mrs. Kehoe's coded warning to Furlong on Christmas Eve in the storage room of her pub, telling him to leave the convent alone. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Them nuns have a finger in every pie, Bill.
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Kehoe
quotes.context: Mrs. Kehoe spelling out the convent's institutional reach in the small town. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: You make a nuisance of yourself you'll be denying your younger ones an education.
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Kehoe
quotes.context: Mrs. Kehoe naming the precise mechanism of community discipline: the convent's secondary school is the only one in town, so crossing the nuns punishes Furlong's daughters. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: They've only as much power as we give them, I thought.
quotes.speaker: Bill Furlong
quotes.context: Furlong's reply to Mrs. Kehoe's warning about the nuns. The first time he names the principle that will lead him to act. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Don't be listening to them, they're only brats.
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Wilson
quotes.context: Mrs. Wilson to young Bill about the schoolchildren who spit on his coat. The kindness that, by Furlong's own argument, made him possible. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: Time is terrible.
quotes.speaker: Ned
quotes.context: Ned to Furlong in Mrs. Wilson's kitchen, talking about how long since she died. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: No need digging up old stories, Bill.
quotes.speaker: Ned
quotes.context: Ned ending Furlong's questions about who his father might be. The line that closes the door Furlong had been trying to push open. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.

quotes.text: And she was a fair woman, treated you like her own, Bill.
quotes.speaker: Ned
quotes.context: Ned's testimony about Mrs. Wilson's care of Furlong as a child. Sets up the moral parallel Furlong will use against Eileen. Source: 2024 screenplay; verify against novel.
